Judge Rosemary Dunbar

FACTS
You recently retired from the Los Angeles Superior Court.
You have not filed a leaving office statement with the clerk of
that court. 
As a retired judge, you will be receiving tenporary
assignrnents to various courts.
ANALYAIS
Section 87200, et seo., requires that judges file statenents
of economic interests (Forn 721t. Hovrever, - regulation L872421
provides that retired judges are not required to file statenents
of econonic interests pursuant to covernnent code Section 872OO,
et seq., if they serve or expect to serve less than 30 days or
rnore in any 12-month period.
Once your assignment ends and you do not receive or
anticipate receiving a new assignnent, you must file a leaving
office statement within 30 days of leaving your last assignnent.
The leaving office statenent wiII be filed with the clerk of the
court of your last assignment. (Cooper Advice Letter, No. 91-O3O.)
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact ne at (915) 322-5662.

sections.question: As a retired judge, are you required to continue filing Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) with each court to which you receive a temporary assignment?
sections.conclusion: Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the superior Court of Los Angeles county and you expect to serve 30 days in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual statements of economic interests. Your statement will be filed with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned at the time you file your statement. If you are not assigned to a specific court at the time you file your statement, you will file your statement with the clerk of the court of your last assignment. When your assignment ends and you do not anticipate receiving a new assignment you must file a leaving office statement within 30 days of the termination of your assignment.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a retired judge must continue filing Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) with courts for temporary assignments after retiring from the Superior Court.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A retired judge who has not filed a leaving office statement and expects to serve 30+ days in a 12-month period must continue filing annual statements of economic interests with the clerk of the court of assignment, and must file a leaving office statement within 30 days when assignments end.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document has OCR quality issues (OCE, seo., ceneral, etc.) but all major sections clearly identifiable. Question and Conclusion sections are clearly marked and verbatim extraction is reliable. Analysis section supports the conclusion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As a retired judge, are you required to continue filing Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) with each court to which you receive a temporary assignment?

CONCLUSION: Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the superior Court of Los Angeles county and you expect to serve 30 days in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual statements of economic interests. Your statement will be filed with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned at the time you file your statement. If you are not assigned to a specific court at the time you file your statement, you will file your statement with the clerk of the court of your last assignment. When your assignment ends and you do not anticipate receiving a new assignment you must file a leaving office statement within 30 days of the termination of your assignment.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Judge Rosemary M. Dunbar that as a retired judge receiving temporary court assignments and expecting to serve at least 30 days in a 12-month period, she must continue filing annual Statements of Economic Interests with the appropriate court clerk and file a leaving office statement within 30 days when her assignments terminate.
